--------------------------------------------------
>> Issue: [B107:hardcoded_password_default] Possible hardcoded password: 'upgrades'
   Severity: Low   Confidence: Medium
   CWE: CWE-259 (https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html)
   More Info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
   Location: ./.env/Lib/python3.10/site-packages/alembic/runtime/environment.py:416:4
415	
416	    def configure(
417	        self,
418	        connection: Optional[Connection] = None,
419	        url: Optional[Union[str, URL]] = None,
420	        dialect_name: Optional[str] = None,
421	        dialect_opts: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
422	        transactional_ddl: Optional[bool] = None,
423	        transaction_per_migration: bool = False,
424	        output_buffer: Optional[TextIO] = None,
425	        starting_rev: Optional[str] = None,
426	        tag: Optional[str] = None,
427	        template_args: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
428	        render_as_batch: bool = False,
429	        target_metadata: Union[MetaData, Sequence[MetaData], None] = None,
430	        include_name: Optional[IncludeNameFn] = None,
431	        include_object: Optional[IncludeObjectFn] = None,
432	        include_schemas: bool = False,
433	        process_revision_directives: Optional[
434	            ProcessRevisionDirectiveFn
435	        ] = None,
436	        compare_type: Union[bool, CompareType] = True,
437	        compare_server_default: Union[bool, CompareServerDefault] = False,
438	        render_item: Optional[RenderItemFn] = None,
439	        literal_binds: bool = False,
440	        upgrade_token: str = "upgrades",
441	        downgrade_token: str = "downgrades",
442	        alembic_module_prefix: str = "op.",
443	        sqlalchemy_module_prefix: str = "sa.",
444	        user_module_prefix: Optional[str] = None,
445	        on_version_apply: Optional[OnVersionApplyFn] = None,
446	        **kw: Any,
447	    ) -> None:
448	        """Configure a :class:`.MigrationContext` within this
449	        :class:`.EnvironmentContext` which will provide database
450	        connectivity and other configuration to a series of
451	        migration scripts.
452	
453	        Many methods on :class:`.EnvironmentContext` require that
454	        this method has been called in order to function, as they
455	        ultimately need to have database access or at least access
456	        to the dialect in use.  Those which do are documented as such.
457	
458	        The important thing needed by :meth:`.configure` is a
459	        means to determine what kind of database dialect is in use.
460	        An actual connection to that database is needed only if
461	        the :class:`.MigrationContext` is to be used in
462	        "online" mode.
463	
464	        If the :meth:`.is_offline_mode` function returns ``True``,
465	        then no connection is needed here.  Otherwise, the
466	        ``connection`` parameter should be present as an
467	        instance of :class:`sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`.
468	
469	        This function is typically called from the ``env.py``
470	        script within a migration environment.  It can be called
471	        multiple times for an invocation.  The most recent
472	        :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
473	        for which it was called is the one that will be operated upon
474	        by the next call to :meth:`.run_migrations`.
475	
476	        General parameters:
477	
478	        :param connection: a :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
479	         to use
480	         for SQL execution in "online" mode.  When present, is also
481	         used to determine the type of dialect in use.
482	        :param url: a string database url, or a
483	         :class:`sqlalchemy.engine.url.URL` object.
484	         The type of dialect to be used will be derived from this if
485	         ``connection`` is not passed.
486	        :param dialect_name: string name of a dialect, such as
487	         "postgresql", "mssql", etc.
488	         The type of dialect to be used will be derived from this if
489	         ``connection`` and ``url`` are not passed.
490	        :param dialect_opts: dictionary of options to be passed to dialect
491	         constructor.
492	        :param transactional_ddl: Force the usage of "transactional"
493	         DDL on or off;
494	         this otherwise defaults to whether or not the dialect in
495	         use supports it.
496	        :param transaction_per_migration: if True, nest each migration script
497	         in a transaction rather than the full series of migrations to
498	         run.
499	        :param output_buffer: a file-like object that will be used
500	         for textual output
501	         when the ``--sql`` option is used to generate SQL scripts.
502	         Defaults to
503	         ``sys.stdout`` if not passed here and also not present on
504	         the :class:`.Config`
505	         object.  The value here overrides that of the :class:`.Config`
506	         object.
507	        :param output_encoding: when using ``--sql`` to generate SQL
508	         scripts, apply this encoding to the string output.
509	        :param literal_binds: when using ``--sql`` to generate SQL
510	         scripts, pass through the ``literal_binds`` flag to the compiler
511	         so that any literal values that would ordinarily be bound
512	         parameters are converted to plain strings.
513	
514	         .. warning:: Dialects can typically only handle simple datatypes
515	            like strings and numbers for auto-literal generation.  Datatypes
516	            like dates, intervals, and others may still require manual
517	            formatting, typically using :meth:`.Operations.inline_literal`.
518	
519	         .. note:: the ``literal_binds`` flag is ignored on SQLAlchemy
520	            versions prior to 0.8 where this feature is not supported.
521	
522	         .. seealso::
523	
524	            :meth:`.Operations.inline_literal`
525	
526	        :param starting_rev: Override the "starting revision" argument
527	         when using ``--sql`` mode.
528	        :param tag: a string tag for usage by custom ``env.py`` scripts.
529	         Set via the ``--tag`` option, can be overridden here.
530	        :param template_args: dictionary of template arguments which
531	         will be added to the template argument environment when
532	         running the "revision" command.   Note that the script environment
533	         is only run within the "revision" command if the --autogenerate
534	         option is used, or if the option "revision_environment=true"
535	         is present in the alembic.ini file.
536	
537	        :param version_table: The name of the Alembic version table.
538	         The default is ``'alembic_version'``.
539	        :param version_table_schema: Optional schema to place version
540	         table within.
541	        :param version_table_pk: boolean, whether the Alembic version table
542	         should use a primary key constraint for the "value" column; this
543	         only takes effect when the table is first created.
544	         Defaults to True; setting to False should not be necessary and is
545	         here for backwards compatibility reasons.
546	        :param on_version_apply: a callable or collection of callables to be
547	            run for each migration step.
548	            The callables will be run in the order they are given, once for
549	            each migration step, after the respective operation has been
550	            applied but before its transaction is finalized.
551	            Each callable accepts no positional arguments and the following
552	            keyword arguments:
553	
554	            * ``ctx``: the :class:`.MigrationContext` running the migration,
555	            * ``step``: a :class:`.MigrationInfo` representing the
556	              step currently being applied,
557	            * ``heads``: a collection of version strings representing the
558	              current heads,
559	            * ``run_args``: the ``**kwargs`` passed to :meth:`.run_migrations`.
560	
561	        Parameters specific to the autogenerate feature, when
562	        ``alembic revision`` is run with the ``--autogenerate`` feature:
563	
564	        :param target_metadata: a :class:`sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
565	         object, or a sequence of :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
566	         objects, that will be consulted during autogeneration.
567	         The tables present in each :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
568	         will be compared against
569	         what is locally available on the target
570	         :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
571	         to produce candidate upgrade/downgrade operations.
572	        :param compare_type: Indicates type comparison behavior during
573	         an autogenerate
574	         operation.  Defaults to ``True`` turning on type comparison, which
575	         has good accuracy on most backends.   See :ref:`compare_types`
576	         for an example as well as information on other type
577	         comparison options. Set to ``False`` which disables type
578	         comparison. A callable can also be passed to provide custom type
579	         comparison, see :ref:`compare_types` for additional details.
580	
581	         .. versionchanged:: 1.12.0 The default value of
582	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_type` has been
583	            changed to ``True``.
584	
585	         .. seealso::
586	
587	            :ref:`compare_types`
588	
589	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_server_default`
590	
591	        :param compare_server_default: Indicates server default comparison
592	         behavior during
593	         an autogenerate operation.  Defaults to ``False`` which disables
594	         server default
595	         comparison.  Set to  ``True`` to turn on server default comparison,
596	         which has
597	         varied accuracy depending on backend.
598	
599	         To customize server default comparison behavior, a callable may
600	         be specified
601	         which can filter server default comparisons during an
602	         autogenerate operation.
603	         defaults during an autogenerate operation.   The format of this
604	         callable is::
605	
606	            def my_compare_server_default(context, inspected_column,
607	                        metadata_column, inspected_default, metadata_default,
608	                        rendered_metadata_default):
609	                # return True if the defaults are different,
610	                # False if not, or None to allow the default implementation
611	                # to compare these defaults
612	                return None
613	
614	            context.configure(
615	                # ...
616	                compare_server_default = my_compare_server_default
617	            )
618	
619	         ``inspected_column`` is a dictionary structure as returned by
620	         :meth:`sqlalchemy.engine.reflection.Inspector.get_columns`, whereas
621	         ``metadata_column`` is a :class:`sqlalchemy.schema.Column` from
622	         the local model environment.
623	
624	         A return value of ``None`` indicates to allow default server default
625	         comparison
626	         to proceed.  Note that some backends such as Postgresql actually
627	         execute
628	         the two defaults on the database side to compare for equivalence.
629	
630	         .. seealso::
631	
632	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_type`
633	
634	        :param include_name: A callable function which is given
635	         the chance to return ``True`` or ``False`` for any database reflected
636	         object based on its name, including database schema names when
637	         the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas` flag
638	         is set to ``True``.
639	
640	         The function accepts the following positional arguments:
641	
642	         * ``name``: the name of the object, such as schema name or table name.
643	           Will be ``None`` when indicating the default schema name of the
644	           database connection.
645	         * ``type``: a string describing the type of object; currently
646	           ``"schema"``, ``"table"``, ``"column"``, ``"index"``,
647	           ``"unique_constraint"``, or ``"foreign_key_constraint"``
648	         * ``parent_names``: a dictionary of "parent" object names, that are
649	           relative to the name being given.  Keys in this dictionary may
650	           include:  ``"schema_name"``, ``"table_name"`` or
651	           ``"schema_qualified_table_name"``.
652	
653	         E.g.::
654	
655	            def include_name(name, type_, parent_names):
656	                if type_ == "schema":
657	                    return name in ["schema_one", "schema_two"]
658	                else:
659	                    return True
660	
661	            context.configure(
662	                # ...
663	                include_schemas = True,
664	                include_name = include_name
665	            )
666	
667	         .. seealso::
668	
669	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
670	
671	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_object`
672	
673	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas`
674	
675	
676	        :param include_object: A callable function which is given
677	         the chance to return ``True`` or ``False`` for any object,
678	         indicating if the given object should be considered in the
679	         autogenerate sweep.
680	
681	         The function accepts the following positional arguments:
682	
683	         * ``object``: a :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.SchemaItem` object such
684	           as a :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table`,
685	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Column`,
686	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Index`
687	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.UniqueConstraint`,
688	           or :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.ForeignKeyConstraint` object
689	         * ``name``: the name of the object. This is typically available
690	           via ``object.name``.
691	         * ``type``: a string describing the type of object; currently
692	           ``"table"``, ``"column"``, ``"index"``, ``"unique_constraint"``,
693	           or ``"foreign_key_constraint"``
694	         * ``reflected``: ``True`` if the given object was produced based on
695	           table reflection, ``False`` if it's from a local :class:`.MetaData`
696	           object.
697	         * ``compare_to``: the object being compared against, if available,
698	           else ``None``.
699	
700	         E.g.::
701	
702	            def include_object(object, name, type_, reflected, compare_to):
703	                if (type_ == "column" and
704	                    not reflected and
705	                    object.info.get("skip_autogenerate", False)):
706	                    return False
707	                else:
708	                    return True
709	
710	            context.configure(
711	                # ...
712	                include_object = include_object
713	            )
714	
715	         For the use case of omitting specific schemas from a target database
716	         when :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas` is
717	         set to ``True``, the :attr:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table.schema`
718	         attribute can be checked for each :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table`
719	         object passed to the hook, however it is much more efficient
720	         to filter on schemas before reflection of objects takes place
721	         using the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
722	         hook.
723	
724	         .. seealso::
725	
726	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
727	
728	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
729	
730	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas`
731	
732	        :param render_as_batch: if True, commands which alter elements
733	         within a table will be placed under a ``with batch_alter_table():``
734	         directive, so that batch migrations will take place.
735	
736	         .. seealso::
737	
738	            :ref:`batch_migrations`
739	
740	        :param include_schemas: If True, autogenerate will scan across
741	         all schemas located by the SQLAlchemy
742	         :meth:`~sqlalchemy.engine.reflection.Inspector.get_schema_names`
743	         method, and include all differences in tables found across all
744	         those schemas.  When using this option, you may want to also
745	         use the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
746	         parameter to specify a callable which
747	         can filter the tables/schemas that get included.
748	
749	         .. seealso::
750	
751	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
752	
753	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
754	
755	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_object`
756	
757	        :param render_item: Callable that can be used to override how
758	         any schema item, i.e. column, constraint, type,
759	         etc., is rendered for autogenerate.  The callable receives a
760	         string describing the type of object, the object, and
761	         the autogen context.  If it returns False, the
762	         default rendering method will be used.  If it returns None,
763	         the item will not be rendered in the context of a Table
764	         construct, that is, can be used to skip columns or constraints
765	         within op.create_table()::
766	
767	            def my_render_column(type_, col, autogen_context):
768	                if type_ == "column" and isinstance(col, MySpecialCol):
769	                    return repr(col)
770	                else:
771	                    return False
772	
773	            context.configure(
774	                # ...
775	                render_item = my_render_column
776	            )
777	
778	         Available values for the type string include: ``"column"``,
779	         ``"primary_key"``, ``"foreign_key"``, ``"unique"``, ``"check"``,
780	         ``"type"``, ``"server_default"``.
781	
782	         .. seealso::
783	
784	            :ref:`autogen_render_types`
785	
786	        :param upgrade_token: When autogenerate completes, the text of the
787	         candidate upgrade operations will be present in this template
788	         variable when ``script.py.mako`` is rendered.  Defaults to
789	         ``upgrades``.
790	        :param downgrade_token: When autogenerate completes, the text of the
791	         candidate downgrade operations will be present in this
792	         template variable when ``script.py.mako`` is rendered.  Defaults to
793	         ``downgrades``.
794	
795	        :param alembic_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to Alembic
796	         :mod:`alembic.operations` constructs, this prefix will be used
797	         (i.e. ``op.create_table``)  Defaults to "``op.``".
798	         Can be ``None`` to indicate no prefix.
799	
800	        :param sqlalchemy_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to
801	         SQLAlchemy
802	         :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Column` or type classes, this prefix
803	         will be used
804	         (i.e. ``sa.Column("somename", sa.Integer)``)  Defaults to "``sa.``".
805	         Can be ``None`` to indicate no prefix.
806	         Note that when dialect-specific types are rendered, autogenerate
807	         will render them using the dialect module name, i.e. ``mssql.BIT()``,
808	         ``postgresql.UUID()``.
809	
810	        :param user_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to a SQLAlchemy
811	         type (e.g. :class:`.TypeEngine`) where the module name is not
812	         under the ``sqlalchemy`` namespace, this prefix will be used
813	         within autogenerate.  If left at its default of
814	         ``None``, the ``__module__`` attribute of the type is used to
815	         render the import module.   It's a good practice to set this
816	         and to have all custom types be available from a fixed module space,
817	         in order to future-proof migration files against reorganizations
818	         in modules.
819	
820	         .. seealso::
821	
822	            :ref:`autogen_module_prefix`
823	
824	        :param process_revision_directives: a callable function that will
825	         be passed a structure representing the end result of an autogenerate
826	         or plain "revision" operation, which can be manipulated to affect
827	         how the ``alembic revision`` command ultimately outputs new
828	         revision scripts.   The structure of the callable is::
829	
830	            def process_revision_directives(context, revision, directives):
831	                pass
832	
833	         The ``directives`` parameter is a Python list containing
834	         a single :class:`.MigrationScript` directive, which represents
835	         the revision file to be generated.    This list as well as its
836	         contents may be freely modified to produce any set of commands.
837	         The section :ref:`customizing_revision` shows an example of
838	         doing this.  The ``context`` parameter is the
839	         :class:`.MigrationContext` in use,
840	         and ``revision`` is a tuple of revision identifiers representing the
841	         current revision of the database.
842	
843	         The callable is invoked at all times when the ``--autogenerate``
844	         option is passed to ``alembic revision``.  If ``--autogenerate``
845	         is not passed, the callable is invoked only if the
846	         ``revision_environment`` variable is set to True in the Alembic
847	         configuration, in which case the given ``directives`` collection
848	         will contain empty :class:`.UpgradeOps` and :class:`.DowngradeOps`
849	         collections for ``.upgrade_ops`` and ``.downgrade_ops``.  The
850	         ``--autogenerate`` option itself can be inferred by inspecting
851	         ``context.config.cmd_opts.autogenerate``.
852	
853	         The callable function may optionally be an instance of
854	         a :class:`.Rewriter` object.  This is a helper object that
855	         assists in the production of autogenerate-stream rewriter functions.
856	
857	         .. seealso::
858	
859	             :ref:`customizing_revision`
860	
861	             :ref:`autogen_rewriter`
862	
863	             :paramref:`.command.revision.process_revision_directives`
864	
865	        Parameters specific to individual backends:
866	
867	        :param mssql_batch_separator: The "batch separator" which will
868	         be placed between each statement when generating offline SQL Server
869	         migrations.  Defaults to ``GO``.  Note this is in addition to the
870	         customary semicolon ``;`` at the end of each statement; SQL Server
871	         considers the "batch separator" to denote the end of an
872	         individual statement execution, and cannot group certain
873	         dependent operations in one step.
874	        :param oracle_batch_separator: The "batch separator" which will
875	         be placed between each statement when generating offline
876	         Oracle migrations.  Defaults to ``/``.  Oracle doesn't add a
877	         semicolon between statements like most other backends.
878	
879	        """
880	        opts = self.context_opts
881	        if transactional_ddl is not None:
882	            opts["transactional_ddl"] = transactional_ddl
883	        if output_buffer is not None:
884	            opts["output_buffer"] = output_buffer
885	        elif self.config.output_buffer is not None:
886	            opts["output_buffer"] = self.config.output_buffer
887	        if starting_rev:
888	            opts["starting_rev"] = starting_rev
889	        if tag:
890	            opts["tag"] = tag
891	        if template_args and "template_args" in opts:
892	            opts["template_args"].update(template_args)
893	        opts["transaction_per_migration"] = transaction_per_migration
894	        opts["target_metadata"] = target_metadata
895	        opts["include_name"] = include_name
896	        opts["include_object"] = include_object
897	        opts["include_schemas"] = include_schemas
898	        opts["render_as_batch"] = render_as_batch
899	        opts["upgrade_token"] = upgrade_token
900	        opts["downgrade_token"] = downgrade_token
901	        opts["sqlalchemy_module_prefix"] = sqlalchemy_module_prefix
902	        opts["alembic_module_prefix"] = alembic_module_prefix
903	        opts["user_module_prefix"] = user_module_prefix
904	        opts["literal_binds"] = literal_binds
905	        opts["process_revision_directives"] = process_revision_directives
906	        opts["on_version_apply"] = util.to_tuple(on_version_apply, default=())
907	
908	        if render_item is not None:
909	            opts["render_item"] = render_item
910	        opts["compare_type"] = compare_type
911	        if compare_server_default is not None:
912	            opts["compare_server_default"] = compare_server_default
913	        opts["script"] = self.script
914	
915	        opts.update(kw)
916	
917	        self._migration_context = MigrationContext.configure(
918	            connection=connection,
919	            url=url,
920	            dialect_name=dialect_name,
921	            environment_context=self,
922	            dialect_opts=dialect_opts,
923	            opts=opts,
924	        )
925

--------------------------------------------------
>> Issue: [B107:hardcoded_password_default] Possible hardcoded password: 'upgrades'
   Severity: Low   Confidence: Medium
   CWE: CWE-259 (https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html)
   More Info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
   Location: ./.env/Lib/site-packages/alembic/runtime/environment.py:416:4
415	
416	    def configure(
417	        self,
418	        connection: Optional[Connection] = None,
419	        url: Optional[Union[str, URL]] = None,
420	        dialect_name: Optional[str] = None,
421	        dialect_opts: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
422	        transactional_ddl: Optional[bool] = None,
423	        transaction_per_migration: bool = False,
424	        output_buffer: Optional[TextIO] = None,
425	        starting_rev: Optional[str] = None,
426	        tag: Optional[str] = None,
427	        template_args: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
428	        render_as_batch: bool = False,
429	        target_metadata: Union[MetaData, Sequence[MetaData], None] = None,
430	        include_name: Optional[IncludeNameFn] = None,
431	        include_object: Optional[IncludeObjectFn] = None,
432	        include_schemas: bool = False,
433	        process_revision_directives: Optional[
434	            ProcessRevisionDirectiveFn
435	        ] = None,
436	        compare_type: Union[bool, CompareType] = True,
437	        compare_server_default: Union[bool, CompareServerDefault] = False,
438	        render_item: Optional[RenderItemFn] = None,
439	        literal_binds: bool = False,
440	        upgrade_token: str = "upgrades",
441	        downgrade_token: str = "downgrades",
442	        alembic_module_prefix: str = "op.",
443	        sqlalchemy_module_prefix: str = "sa.",
444	        user_module_prefix: Optional[str] = None,
445	        on_version_apply: Optional[OnVersionApplyFn] = None,
446	        **kw: Any,
447	    ) -> None:
448	        """Configure a :class:`.MigrationContext` within this
449	        :class:`.EnvironmentContext` which will provide database
450	        connectivity and other configuration to a series of
451	        migration scripts.
452	
453	        Many methods on :class:`.EnvironmentContext` require that
454	        this method has been called in order to function, as they
455	        ultimately need to have database access or at least access
456	        to the dialect in use.  Those which do are documented as such.
457	
458	        The important thing needed by :meth:`.configure` is a
459	        means to determine what kind of database dialect is in use.
460	        An actual connection to that database is needed only if
461	        the :class:`.MigrationContext` is to be used in
462	        "online" mode.
463	
464	        If the :meth:`.is_offline_mode` function returns ``True``,
465	        then no connection is needed here.  Otherwise, the
466	        ``connection`` parameter should be present as an
467	        instance of :class:`sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`.
468	
469	        This function is typically called from the ``env.py``
470	        script within a migration environment.  It can be called
471	        multiple times for an invocation.  The most recent
472	        :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
473	        for which it was called is the one that will be operated upon
474	        by the next call to :meth:`.run_migrations`.
475	
476	        General parameters:
477	
478	        :param connection: a :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
479	         to use
480	         for SQL execution in "online" mode.  When present, is also
481	         used to determine the type of dialect in use.
482	        :param url: a string database url, or a
483	         :class:`sqlalchemy.engine.url.URL` object.
484	         The type of dialect to be used will be derived from this if
485	         ``connection`` is not passed.
486	        :param dialect_name: string name of a dialect, such as
487	         "postgresql", "mssql", etc.
488	         The type of dialect to be used will be derived from this if
489	         ``connection`` and ``url`` are not passed.
490	        :param dialect_opts: dictionary of options to be passed to dialect
491	         constructor.
492	        :param transactional_ddl: Force the usage of "transactional"
493	         DDL on or off;
494	         this otherwise defaults to whether or not the dialect in
495	         use supports it.
496	        :param transaction_per_migration: if True, nest each migration script
497	         in a transaction rather than the full series of migrations to
498	         run.
499	        :param output_buffer: a file-like object that will be used
500	         for textual output
501	         when the ``--sql`` option is used to generate SQL scripts.
502	         Defaults to
503	         ``sys.stdout`` if not passed here and also not present on
504	         the :class:`.Config`
505	         object.  The value here overrides that of the :class:`.Config`
506	         object.
507	        :param output_encoding: when using ``--sql`` to generate SQL
508	         scripts, apply this encoding to the string output.
509	        :param literal_binds: when using ``--sql`` to generate SQL
510	         scripts, pass through the ``literal_binds`` flag to the compiler
511	         so that any literal values that would ordinarily be bound
512	         parameters are converted to plain strings.
513	
514	         .. warning:: Dialects can typically only handle simple datatypes
515	            like strings and numbers for auto-literal generation.  Datatypes
516	            like dates, intervals, and others may still require manual
517	            formatting, typically using :meth:`.Operations.inline_literal`.
518	
519	         .. note:: the ``literal_binds`` flag is ignored on SQLAlchemy
520	            versions prior to 0.8 where this feature is not supported.
521	
522	         .. seealso::
523	
524	            :meth:`.Operations.inline_literal`
525	
526	        :param starting_rev: Override the "starting revision" argument
527	         when using ``--sql`` mode.
528	        :param tag: a string tag for usage by custom ``env.py`` scripts.
529	         Set via the ``--tag`` option, can be overridden here.
530	        :param template_args: dictionary of template arguments which
531	         will be added to the template argument environment when
532	         running the "revision" command.   Note that the script environment
533	         is only run within the "revision" command if the --autogenerate
534	         option is used, or if the option "revision_environment=true"
535	         is present in the alembic.ini file.
536	
537	        :param version_table: The name of the Alembic version table.
538	         The default is ``'alembic_version'``.
539	        :param version_table_schema: Optional schema to place version
540	         table within.
541	        :param version_table_pk: boolean, whether the Alembic version table
542	         should use a primary key constraint for the "value" column; this
543	         only takes effect when the table is first created.
544	         Defaults to True; setting to False should not be necessary and is
545	         here for backwards compatibility reasons.
546	        :param on_version_apply: a callable or collection of callables to be
547	            run for each migration step.
548	            The callables will be run in the order they are given, once for
549	            each migration step, after the respective operation has been
550	            applied but before its transaction is finalized.
551	            Each callable accepts no positional arguments and the following
552	            keyword arguments:
553	
554	            * ``ctx``: the :class:`.MigrationContext` running the migration,
555	            * ``step``: a :class:`.MigrationInfo` representing the
556	              step currently being applied,
557	            * ``heads``: a collection of version strings representing the
558	              current heads,
559	            * ``run_args``: the ``**kwargs`` passed to :meth:`.run_migrations`.
560	
561	        Parameters specific to the autogenerate feature, when
562	        ``alembic revision`` is run with the ``--autogenerate`` feature:
563	
564	        :param target_metadata: a :class:`sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
565	         object, or a sequence of :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
566	         objects, that will be consulted during autogeneration.
567	         The tables present in each :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
568	         will be compared against
569	         what is locally available on the target
570	         :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
571	         to produce candidate upgrade/downgrade operations.
572	        :param compare_type: Indicates type comparison behavior during
573	         an autogenerate
574	         operation.  Defaults to ``True`` turning on type comparison, which
575	         has good accuracy on most backends.   See :ref:`compare_types`
576	         for an example as well as information on other type
577	         comparison options. Set to ``False`` which disables type
578	         comparison. A callable can also be passed to provide custom type
579	         comparison, see :ref:`compare_types` for additional details.
580	
581	         .. versionchanged:: 1.12.0 The default value of
582	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_type` has been
583	            changed to ``True``.
584	
585	         .. seealso::
586	
587	            :ref:`compare_types`
588	
589	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_server_default`
590	
591	        :param compare_server_default: Indicates server default comparison
592	         behavior during
593	         an autogenerate operation.  Defaults to ``False`` which disables
594	         server default
595	         comparison.  Set to  ``True`` to turn on server default comparison,
596	         which has
597	         varied accuracy depending on backend.
598	
599	         To customize server default comparison behavior, a callable may
600	         be specified
601	         which can filter server default comparisons during an
602	         autogenerate operation.
603	         defaults during an autogenerate operation.   The format of this
604	         callable is::
605	
606	            def my_compare_server_default(context, inspected_column,
607	                        metadata_column, inspected_default, metadata_default,
608	                        rendered_metadata_default):
609	                # return True if the defaults are different,
610	                # False if not, or None to allow the default implementation
611	                # to compare these defaults
612	                return None
613	
614	            context.configure(
615	                # ...
616	                compare_server_default = my_compare_server_default
617	            )
618	
619	         ``inspected_column`` is a dictionary structure as returned by
620	         :meth:`sqlalchemy.engine.reflection.Inspector.get_columns`, whereas
621	         ``metadata_column`` is a :class:`sqlalchemy.schema.Column` from
622	         the local model environment.
623	
624	         A return value of ``None`` indicates to allow default server default
625	         comparison
626	         to proceed.  Note that some backends such as Postgresql actually
627	         execute
628	         the two defaults on the database side to compare for equivalence.
629	
630	         .. seealso::
631	
632	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_type`
633	
634	        :param include_name: A callable function which is given
635	         the chance to return ``True`` or ``False`` for any database reflected
636	         object based on its name, including database schema names when
637	         the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas` flag
638	         is set to ``True``.
639	
640	         The function accepts the following positional arguments:
641	
642	         * ``name``: the name of the object, such as schema name or table name.
643	           Will be ``None`` when indicating the default schema name of the
644	           database connection.
645	         * ``type``: a string describing the type of object; currently
646	           ``"schema"``, ``"table"``, ``"column"``, ``"index"``,
647	           ``"unique_constraint"``, or ``"foreign_key_constraint"``
648	         * ``parent_names``: a dictionary of "parent" object names, that are
649	           relative to the name being given.  Keys in this dictionary may
650	           include:  ``"schema_name"``, ``"table_name"`` or
651	           ``"schema_qualified_table_name"``.
652	
653	         E.g.::
654	
655	            def include_name(name, type_, parent_names):
656	                if type_ == "schema":
657	                    return name in ["schema_one", "schema_two"]
658	                else:
659	                    return True
660	
661	            context.configure(
662	                # ...
663	                include_schemas = True,
664	                include_name = include_name
665	            )
666	
667	         .. seealso::
668	
669	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
670	
671	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_object`
672	
673	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas`
674	
675	
676	        :param include_object: A callable function which is given
677	         the chance to return ``True`` or ``False`` for any object,
678	         indicating if the given object should be considered in the
679	         autogenerate sweep.
680	
681	         The function accepts the following positional arguments:
682	
683	         * ``object``: a :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.SchemaItem` object such
684	           as a :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table`,
685	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Column`,
686	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Index`
687	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.UniqueConstraint`,
688	           or :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.ForeignKeyConstraint` object
689	         * ``name``: the name of the object. This is typically available
690	           via ``object.name``.
691	         * ``type``: a string describing the type of object; currently
692	           ``"table"``, ``"column"``, ``"index"``, ``"unique_constraint"``,
693	           or ``"foreign_key_constraint"``
694	         * ``reflected``: ``True`` if the given object was produced based on
695	           table reflection, ``False`` if it's from a local :class:`.MetaData`
696	           object.
697	         * ``compare_to``: the object being compared against, if available,
698	           else ``None``.
699	
700	         E.g.::
701	
702	            def include_object(object, name, type_, reflected, compare_to):
703	                if (type_ == "column" and
704	                    not reflected and
705	                    object.info.get("skip_autogenerate", False)):
706	                    return False
707	                else:
708	                    return True
709	
710	            context.configure(
711	                # ...
712	                include_object = include_object
713	            )
714	
715	         For the use case of omitting specific schemas from a target database
716	         when :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas` is
717	         set to ``True``, the :attr:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table.schema`
718	         attribute can be checked for each :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table`
719	         object passed to the hook, however it is much more efficient
720	         to filter on schemas before reflection of objects takes place
721	         using the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
722	         hook.
723	
724	         .. seealso::
725	
726	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
727	
728	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
729	
730	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas`
731	
732	        :param render_as_batch: if True, commands which alter elements
733	         within a table will be placed under a ``with batch_alter_table():``
734	         directive, so that batch migrations will take place.
735	
736	         .. seealso::
737	
738	            :ref:`batch_migrations`
739	
740	        :param include_schemas: If True, autogenerate will scan across
741	         all schemas located by the SQLAlchemy
742	         :meth:`~sqlalchemy.engine.reflection.Inspector.get_schema_names`
743	         method, and include all differences in tables found across all
744	         those schemas.  When using this option, you may want to also
745	         use the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
746	         parameter to specify a callable which
747	         can filter the tables/schemas that get included.
748	
749	         .. seealso::
750	
751	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
752	
753	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
754	
755	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_object`
756	
757	        :param render_item: Callable that can be used to override how
758	         any schema item, i.e. column, constraint, type,
759	         etc., is rendered for autogenerate.  The callable receives a
760	         string describing the type of object, the object, and
761	         the autogen context.  If it returns False, the
762	         default rendering method will be used.  If it returns None,
763	         the item will not be rendered in the context of a Table
764	         construct, that is, can be used to skip columns or constraints
765	         within op.create_table()::
766	
767	            def my_render_column(type_, col, autogen_context):
768	                if type_ == "column" and isinstance(col, MySpecialCol):
769	                    return repr(col)
770	                else:
771	                    return False
772	
773	            context.configure(
774	                # ...
775	                render_item = my_render_column
776	            )
777	
778	         Available values for the type string include: ``"column"``,
779	         ``"primary_key"``, ``"foreign_key"``, ``"unique"``, ``"check"``,
780	         ``"type"``, ``"server_default"``.
781	
782	         .. seealso::
783	
784	            :ref:`autogen_render_types`
785	
786	        :param upgrade_token: When autogenerate completes, the text of the
787	         candidate upgrade operations will be present in this template
788	         variable when ``script.py.mako`` is rendered.  Defaults to
789	         ``upgrades``.
790	        :param downgrade_token: When autogenerate completes, the text of the
791	         candidate downgrade operations will be present in this
792	         template variable when ``script.py.mako`` is rendered.  Defaults to
793	         ``downgrades``.
794	
795	        :param alembic_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to Alembic
796	         :mod:`alembic.operations` constructs, this prefix will be used
797	         (i.e. ``op.create_table``)  Defaults to "``op.``".
798	         Can be ``None`` to indicate no prefix.
799	
800	        :param sqlalchemy_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to
801	         SQLAlchemy
802	         :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Column` or type classes, this prefix
803	         will be used
804	         (i.e. ``sa.Column("somename", sa.Integer)``)  Defaults to "``sa.``".
805	         Can be ``None`` to indicate no prefix.
806	         Note that when dialect-specific types are rendered, autogenerate
807	         will render them using the dialect module name, i.e. ``mssql.BIT()``,
808	         ``postgresql.UUID()``.
809	
810	        :param user_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to a SQLAlchemy
811	         type (e.g. :class:`.TypeEngine`) where the module name is not
812	         under the ``sqlalchemy`` namespace, this prefix will be used
813	         within autogenerate.  If left at its default of
814	         ``None``, the ``__module__`` attribute of the type is used to
815	         render the import module.   It's a good practice to set this
816	         and to have all custom types be available from a fixed module space,
817	         in order to future-proof migration files against reorganizations
818	         in modules.
819	
820	         .. seealso::
821	
822	            :ref:`autogen_module_prefix`
823	
824	        :param process_revision_directives: a callable function that will
825	         be passed a structure representing the end result of an autogenerate
826	         or plain "revision" operation, which can be manipulated to affect
827	         how the ``alembic revision`` command ultimately outputs new
828	         revision scripts.   The structure of the callable is::
829	
830	            def process_revision_directives(context, revision, directives):
831	                pass
832	
833	         The ``directives`` parameter is a Python list containing
834	         a single :class:`.MigrationScript` directive, which represents
835	         the revision file to be generated.    This list as well as its
836	         contents may be freely modified to produce any set of commands.
837	         The section :ref:`customizing_revision` shows an example of
838	         doing this.  The ``context`` parameter is the
839	         :class:`.MigrationContext` in use,
840	         and ``revision`` is a tuple of revision identifiers representing the
841	         current revision of the database.
842	
843	         The callable is invoked at all times when the ``--autogenerate``
844	         option is passed to ``alembic revision``.  If ``--autogenerate``
845	         is not passed, the callable is invoked only if the
846	         ``revision_environment`` variable is set to True in the Alembic
847	         configuration, in which case the given ``directives`` collection
848	         will contain empty :class:`.UpgradeOps` and :class:`.DowngradeOps`
849	         collections for ``.upgrade_ops`` and ``.downgrade_ops``.  The
850	         ``--autogenerate`` option itself can be inferred by inspecting
851	         ``context.config.cmd_opts.autogenerate``.
852	
853	         The callable function may optionally be an instance of
854	         a :class:`.Rewriter` object.  This is a helper object that
855	         assists in the production of autogenerate-stream rewriter functions.
856	
857	         .. seealso::
858	
859	             :ref:`customizing_revision`
860	
861	             :ref:`autogen_rewriter`
862	
863	             :paramref:`.command.revision.process_revision_directives`
864	
865	        Parameters specific to individual backends:
866	
867	        :param mssql_batch_separator: The "batch separator" which will
868	         be placed between each statement when generating offline SQL Server
869	         migrations.  Defaults to ``GO``.  Note this is in addition to the
870	         customary semicolon ``;`` at the end of each statement; SQL Server
871	         considers the "batch separator" to denote the end of an
872	         individual statement execution, and cannot group certain
873	         dependent operations in one step.
874	        :param oracle_batch_separator: The "batch separator" which will
875	         be placed between each statement when generating offline
876	         Oracle migrations.  Defaults to ``/``.  Oracle doesn't add a
877	         semicolon between statements like most other backends.
878	
879	        """
880	        opts = self.context_opts
881	        if transactional_ddl is not None:
882	            opts["transactional_ddl"] = transactional_ddl
883	        if output_buffer is not None:
884	            opts["output_buffer"] = output_buffer
885	        elif self.config.output_buffer is not None:
886	            opts["output_buffer"] = self.config.output_buffer
887	        if starting_rev:
888	            opts["starting_rev"] = starting_rev
889	        if tag:
890	            opts["tag"] = tag
891	        if template_args and "template_args" in opts:
892	            opts["template_args"].update(template_args)
893	        opts["transaction_per_migration"] = transaction_per_migration
894	        opts["target_metadata"] = target_metadata
895	        opts["include_name"] = include_name
896	        opts["include_object"] = include_object
897	        opts["include_schemas"] = include_schemas
898	        opts["render_as_batch"] = render_as_batch
899	        opts["upgrade_token"] = upgrade_token
900	        opts["downgrade_token"] = downgrade_token
901	        opts["sqlalchemy_module_prefix"] = sqlalchemy_module_prefix
902	        opts["alembic_module_prefix"] = alembic_module_prefix
903	        opts["user_module_prefix"] = user_module_prefix
904	        opts["literal_binds"] = literal_binds
905	        opts["process_revision_directives"] = process_revision_directives
906	        opts["on_version_apply"] = util.to_tuple(on_version_apply, default=())
907	
908	        if render_item is not None:
909	            opts["render_item"] = render_item
910	        opts["compare_type"] = compare_type
911	        if compare_server_default is not None:
912	            opts["compare_server_default"] = compare_server_default
913	        opts["script"] = self.script
914	
915	        opts.update(kw)
916	
917	        self._migration_context = MigrationContext.configure(
918	            connection=connection,
919	            url=url,
920	            dialect_name=dialect_name,
921	            environment_context=self,
922	            dialect_opts=dialect_opts,
923	            opts=opts,
924	        )
925

42	
43	    query_frecuencia = text(f"""
44	        SELECT 
45	            s.nombre_servicio, 
46	            COUNT(dc.id_detalle_cita) AS veces_realizado,
47	            SUM(dc.subtotal) AS monto_generado
48	        FROM detalle_cita dc
49	        JOIN servicio s ON dc.id_servicio = s.id_servicio
50	        JOIN cita c ON dc.id_cita = c.id_cita
51	        JOIN pago p ON c.id_cita = p.id_cita
52	        WHERE {condicion_pago}
53	        GROUP BY s.id_servicio, s.nombre_servicio
54	        ORDER BY veces_realizado DESC
55	    """)

59	        SELECT 
60	            prod.nombre AS producto,
61	            SUM(ins.cantidad_utilizada) AS cantidad_total,
62	            um.nombre_unidad
63	        FROM detalle_cita dc
